title: [lichen_l2.c:596] lichen_link_tx dst_eui64=NULL hardcoded for broadcast
description:
Gap found: TX destination addressing not reviewed.

Why this was missed: Round 1 focused on error paths, not broadcast assumption.

The actual bug: Line 596 calls lichen_link_tx() with NULL for dst_eui64:
  lichen_link_tx(&link_ctx, tx_ipv6_buf, pkt_len, NULL, tx_frame_buf, &frame_len)

The comment says 'NULL dst_eui64 = broadcast (no destination address in frame header)'.

This means LICHEN ALWAYS sends broadcast frames. There's no support for:
- Unicast to specific peer (would save airtime in multi-hop scenarios)
- Directed addressing (needed for certain RPL modes)

This is a design limitation, not a bug. The IPv6 destination address IS in the packet (after SCHC decompression), but the L2 frame itself is always broadcast.

For a mesh network this is actually correct - you want all neighbors to hear the frame for routing purposes. But the code has no way to do L2 unicast if needed.

Priority 4 - design documentation, current behavior is intentional.
close_reason: Fixed: Added documentation comment explaining the broadcast-only L2 TX design decision. NULL dst_eui64 is intentional for mesh operation; explains rationale and notes how to extend for L2 unicast if needed in the future.
